n.real_verified_against: .codex-tmp/ocr/*写作*.txt + rendered writing-screen PNGs (.codex-tmp/bs_render*/, 6 PDFs @ zoom 3.5)

measurer: scripts/research/bs_measure.mjs (detectors hand-validated against recalled targets AND rendered screens; --dump <dim>)
notes: The on-screen task is a 2-speaker chat: speaker-1 says one conversational line (usually a question to 'you' or a 1st-person setup), the test-taker's reply is a row of blank slots with 1-2 PREFILLED anchor words (start fragment / mid word / end tail w/ punctuation), and a pool of draggable word-tiles below. Instruction is the constant 'Make an appropriate sentence.' CRITICAL render-level finding: across 14 items rendered from 6 exams, the word-pool has NO distractor tile — every tile maps into the answer (the OCR 'did/op/Qing/oing/p!p' tokens are UI chrome / OCR noise). This contradicts the in-code note in etsProfile.js (82% tile-level distractor) and is the top reconcile item — see deferred[0]. Real BS is campus small-talk in the first person: 40% first-person answers, 23% contractions, 10% casual openers, 63% campus topic vs 0.3% office. The generator drifts formal/3rd-person/office and over-traps with distractors (89%, of which 'did' x160).

dimensions.name: answer_length_words
examples: it wasn't available online . (4w)
which brand are you considering ? (5w)
I do not usually go to those events . (9w)
my professor also asked me how I was able to complete it ahead of time (14w)
dimensions.gap: Gen runs ~1.5w long (mean 10.7 vs 9.16) and never produces a 4-6 word answer (real has 72 such = 14%). Gen floor is 7w.

dimensions.name: difficulty_mix_length_proxy
dimensions.detector: word-count bands <=7 / 8-11 / >=12 (proxy; true difficulty also = structure, see correlations)
examples: easy: 'I have a conflicting appointment.'
hard: 'Can you tell me whether it will reopen by the end of the month?'
dimensions.gap: Gen ~1% easy vs 25% real, and 2x the hard band. Over-complexifies both ends. Targets in ETS_DIFFICULTY_RATIO (22/60/18) are correct; generator does not obey them.

dimensions.name: sentence_type_4way
dimensions.unit: share of answers by (question-form x embedded-clause)
dimensions.detector: classifyType() = isQuestionForm (terminal ? or inverted main clause) x hasEmbedded (know/tell/ask/think/wonder/not-sure + if/whether/wh); --dump types. Validated vs full 2.8/2.23/2.25 sets.
examples: q-embedded: do you know if the due dates have been updated?
q-plain: what time does the game start?
decl-indirect: He wanted to know how I did my research.
statement: I worked with a company that aimed to reduce carbon emissions.
dimensions.gap: Gen INVERTS the question structure: makes the embedded clause declarative ('She wanted to know if…' 20% vs real 6%) and almost never a question ('Do you know if…?' 6.5% vs real 18%); plain direct Qs 2.4% vs real 17%. Real's two question types (35% combined) are 9% in gen.

dimensions.name: qmark_reply_ends_question
dimensions.detector: /\?\s*$/ (undercounts true questions: many recalled targets dropped terminal ?; sentence_type question-FORM is the truer 35%)
examples: have you applied for it yet?
which brand are you considering?
dimensions.gap: Gen slightly low (9% vs 14.5%); TPO_REFERENCE_PROFILE.qmarkRatio 0.14 is correct, generator under.

dimensions.name: signature_do_you_know_if
dimensions.unit: share = 'Do you know if/whether…?' / 'Can you tell me whether…?'
dimensions.detector: isSignature() = ^(do you know|can you tell me|could you tell me|do you think it/that) (if|whether|wh-)
examples: do you know if she included the schedule?
Can you tell me whether he provided a reason?
do you know when they will announce the results?
dimensions.gap: The single most recognizable real-exam BS pattern, under-produced ~3x. Add explicit '~1 in 6 items is a polite Do-you-know-if / Can-you-tell-me-whether indirect request' to genPrompt.

dimensions.name: negation
examples: I haven't received any emails today.
I do not usually go to those events.
Unfortunately, I did not have time for a visit.
dimensions.gap: Rate matches (negligible). BUT prompt target 'negation ~9%' and negationRatio 0.2 BOTH undershoot real 24% — declining/excusing is a stock reply. Also gen's negations skew formal/3P (see register). Raise prompt target to ~20-24%.

dimensions.name: relative_clause
dimensions.unit: share of answers with a relative clause
dimensions.detector: isRelative() (that/which/who + verb, or 'the one that/where'); all 93 hand-confirmed genuine relatives
examples: I'm enrolled in the one that covers advanced mathematics.
I worked with a company that aimed to reduce carbon emissions.
the park that has the best trails is my favourite.
it's the one that has the software that I need for my project.
dimensions.gap: ~7x under-produced. The 'the one that…/the place where…' reply (describing a choice among options) is a core real pattern almost absent from gen, and it supplies most of the hard band. Prompt says 'relative 0-1 per set' — too low; bump to ~2/10.

dimensions.name: passive_voice
examples: real (light, inside embedded clause): 'do you know if the due dates have been updated?'
gen (heavy, standalone): 'The customer did not understand why the new policy was implemented.'
dimensions.gap: Gen 2x the passive and clunkier/formal. Keep passive light and embedded; don't open with heavy agentless passive.

dimensions.name: register_contractions
dimensions.unit: share of answers with a contraction
examples: it wasn't available online.
I'm practicing the piece that my professor suggested.
I don't think I can make it this time.
dimensions.gap: Gen 3x less contracted -> reads written/formal not spoken. No current prompt line addresses register; add 'answers are casual spoken replies; use contractions freely'.

dimensions.name: register_casual_opener
examples: Sorry, but I have not had time to look at it yet.
No, I haven't had a chance to buy it.
Unfortunately, I never had a chance to read the book.
That's right. I am not sure what I want to do in the future.
dimensions.gap: Gen essentially never frames the reply as answering someone. Add: ~1 in 10 replies opens Yes/No/Sorry/Unfortunately (also becomes a START prefilled anchor).

dimensions.name: register_subject_person
examples: real first-person: 'I had to finish my economics assignment.'
gen formal-3P (never in real): 'The project supervisor could not locate the budget file.'
gen formal-3P (never in real): 'The frustrated customer wanted to know why the delivery was so late.'
dimensions.gap: Gen halves first-person voice and invents bureaucratic 3P personas the real exam never uses. Reply should usually be the test-taker speaking ('I…'). Demote invented-persona answers.

dimensions.name: distractor_presence
dimensions.unit: share of items with a distractor tile
dimensions.detector: real: visual tile-vs-answer-word count on rendered screens (structured JSON has no reliable distractor field). current: item.distractor non-empty; --dump distractor
examples: real 2.23 Q1: pool [take | the workshop | was | that was | supposed to | place] -> ALL used in 'the workshop that was supposed to take place was canceled' (prefilled 'canceled.'); no extra tile.
gen: distractor 'did' on 160 items, e.g. chunks [needed,to,find,out,where,usually waits,for packages,'did',he]
dimensions.gap: #1 GAP. Recalled exam supplies exact tile set (no trap); gen adds a trap to ~9/10 items, 19% of them the word 'did'. CAVEAT: contradicts etsProfile.js note (82% tile-level distractor on recalled) — must reconcile (deferred[0]) before changing the target. Until then, 'distractor on 89% / did x160' is a known over-trap regardless.
dimensions.reliability: solid-for-recalled-render-sample; target deferred pending official-vs-recalled reconcile

dimensions.name: chunk_tile_structure
dimensions.unit: effective tiles per item / single-word-tile share / words per tile

real.note: renders show multi-word tiles exist (the workshop / that was / supposed to / an individual interview / will be online or / park that / is close); true single-word ratio NOT recoverable from OCR

dimensions.detector: current: chunks.length - (distractor?1:0); single-word ratio over all chunks. real: visual on renders (boundaries lost in OCR)
examples: real multi-word tiles: 'the workshop', 'that was', 'supposed to', 'an individual interview', 'will be online or'
dimensions.gap: Effective tile COUNT in range (5.87 vs real ~5-7). Single-word ratio: prompt targets ~77% single-word but renders show plenty of 2-3-word tiles, so the 77% target may be too high — unverifiable against real (OCR can't recover boundaries).

dimensions.name: prefilled_presence_placement
dimensions.unit: presence share / two-anchor share / mean segment words
dimensions.detector: real: OCR-block split (prompt=first line, pool=longest jumbled line, rest=prefilled), anchored to renders. current: prefilled[] length / positions
examples: real START: 'She wanted' / 'Can you' / 'It has' / 'I just read'
real MID: 'know' / 'you' / 'which' / 'decided'
real END tail (w/ punctuation): 'canceled.' / 'favorite spot.' / 'seafood dishes.' / 'classmate?' / 'be?'
real TWO anchors: 'Can you' + '…?'; 'I'm sorry, but' + '.'
dimensions.gap: Gen almost never uses TWO anchors (1.1% vs real ~21%) and rarely a punctuation-bearing END TAIL ('canceled.'). Presence slightly low (76.6% vs ~85%). PREFILLED_PROFILE.multiSegmentRatio 0.30 ~ real 21%; add explicit END-tail guidance.

dimensions.name: prefilled_not_subject
dimensions.unit: share where prefilled hint is a bare subject pronoun

real.note: renders: prefilled is usually verb-phrase/mid-word/end-tail, ~2/14 were a person subject; consistent with prompt's TPO 30%

examples: TPO-style anchor a NON-subject: prefilled 'wanted to know' not 'she'
dimensions.gap: Holding within target (23.2% <= 30%). The earlier-fixed 'subject-as-hint' regression is not recurring. Keep the rule.

dimensions.name: prompt_addresses_you
dimensions.unit: share of conversational prompts containing you/your
dimensions.detector: real: pickRealPrompt() (OCR-gluing-robust) + no-space you/your test. current: /you|your/ on prompt field.
examples: Why didn't you go to the class yesterday?
What is your favorite place to relax?
Did you submit the assignment on time?
dimensions.gap: Gen prompts are detached 3rd-person scene reports half the time ('What did the store manager report…') instead of speaking to the student. Prompt's '⚡ #1 RULE you/your ~6-7/10' target (76%) is correct; generator at 48% not obeying.

dimensions.name: prompt_opener_mix
dimensions.unit: share of prompts by opener type
dimensions.detector: real: classifyRealOpener() (no-space opener regex + terminal-? check). current: popener() on prompt field.
examples: otherWh (dominant): 'Why didn't you answer your phone?' / 'Where did you get that information?'
yes/no: 'Are you planning to attend the workshop on Saturday?'
whatX (rare in real): 'What did the instructor say about the exam format?'
statement setup: "I'm preparing for my presentation on Friday."
dimensions.gap: Gen mix is nearly INVERTED: over-uses 'What did…?' (54% vs real 9%), under-uses other-wh (14% vs 45%) and yes/no (8% vs 31%). Prompt's 'TPO 36/24/18/18' target is wrong for 2026改后; recalibrate to otherWh~45 / yesno~31 / whatX~9 / statement~8 and cap 'What did…?' hard.

dimensions.name: topic_domain
dimensions.detector: keyword domains on full prompt+pool blob (real) / answer+prompt (current). Broader office lexicon -> ~28% current.
examples: real campus: class/lecture/professor/assignment/workshop/library/roommate
gen office (rare in real): store manager/shipment/budget file/quarterly report
dimensions.gap: Real BS is overwhelmingly campus life (63% vs 0.3% office); gen dilutes with workplace scenarios that produce the formal personas (register_subject_person). Anchor scenarios to campus/student life; drop office/corporate.

correlations: Difficulty = length x structure, jointly. The easy band (<=7w, 25%) is mostly plain first-person statements & short yes/no questions; the hard band (>=12w, 16%) is mostly embedded-question ('Can you tell me whether…') and relative-clause sentences. The generator misses 'easy' precisely because it avoids short plain statements and over-uses multi-clause/formal constructions. To hit 25% easy, generate short PLAIN first-person statements; the 18% relative target naturally supplies the hard band.

Register, voice, and topic are ONE knob. First-person (40%) <-> contractions (23%) <-> casual openers (10%) <-> campus topic (63%) co-vary: a casual student reply about their own class life is contracted, first-person, and often opens 'Sorry,/No,'. The gen office personas are exactly the formal, un-contracted, third-person items. Fixing topic->campus and voice->first-person pulls contractions and casual openers up for free.

Anchor choice brackets the hard middle. Items opening 'Sorry, but…' / 'No, but you' give that opener as the START prefilled; items ending in a content noun ('…favorite spot.', '…classmate?') give that as the END tail. Generating the anchor from the sentence's natural opener/closer also yields the two-segment prefilled pattern (21% real) the generator lacks (1.1%).
deferred: DISTRACTOR DENSITY — official vs recalled (CRITICAL). This spec finds 0 distractor tiles in 14 rendered recalled items; etsProfile.js (lines 26-33) records a deliberate decision to keep ~88% density citing a tile-level recalled re-measure of 49/60=82% and ETS Full-Length Practice at 2/20=10% (data/buildSentence/tpo_official.json). 0% / 82% / 10% cannot all be right. Action: render ~20 more recalled BS screens across more dates, count tiles-vs-answer-words per item (scripts/ops/measure-bs-distractor-bysource.mjs), and settle whether real items ship a trap tile and how often. Strongest single lever on authenticity — target deferred until reconciled.

Chunk single-word ratio for the REAL bank — OCR destroys tile-boundary spacing; renders confirm 2-3-word tiles exist but n=14 is too small for a ratio. The 'so-77% single-word' prompt target is unverified against real. Harden by hand-transcribing tile boundaries from ~30 rendered pools.

Prefilled fine TYPE distribution — presence (~85%), two-anchor (~21%), END-tail prevalence are anchored to 14 renders + 40 clean OCR blocks; the 7-way word-TYPE split in PREFILLED_PROFILE is inherited from older TPO analysis and NOT re-measured against 2026 renders. More transcribed renders would replace it with measured 2026 numbers.

Official-tier ground truth — all 504 targets are recalled-tier (reconstructed by test-takers). UI structure is render-verified but exact wording, terminal punctuation, and the distractor question are recalled-quality. Treat type/length/register ratios as strong signal; distractor-absence as strong-for-recalled-but-unconfirmed-for-official.

Prompt opener mix precision — 84/363 prompts too OCR-garbled to classify; the 298 classified slightly favor well-OCR'd (often shorter) prompts. Direction (otherWh >> whatX) is robust; exact percentages +/-3-4pts.
